scenes.chain_of_thought:
STEP 1 — REASONING:

For each geometry prim write ONE line:
  "Prim <path>: material=<X> because <1-2 word visual cue>. Confidence:<level>."
  (Mass is computed automatically from bbox — you do NOT need to calculate it.)

Prim /anymal_c/Geometry/base: material=steel because silvery grey, structural. Confidence:high.

For each joint prim write 2-3 sentences (full reasoning required for joint validation):
  Revolute: "Joint <name>: lower=<X>°, upper=<Y>°. <reason why valid/invalid>.
   Corrected: lower=<A>°, upper=<B>°. Confidence:<level>."
  Prismatic: "Joint <name>: lower=<X>, upper=<Y>, body_length_along_axis=<B>.
   travel=upper−lower=<T>. <T> vs <B>: <exceeds→VIOLATED, joint_valid=false|fits→valid, joint_valid=true>.
   Corrected if needed: lower=<A>, upper=<C>. Confidence:<level>."

Joint /anymal_c/Geometry/base/LF_HIP/LF_THIGH/LF_SHANK/LF_KFE: Revolute joint with lower=-Infinity, upper=Infinity. Human-like joints typically have limited range, but this is an industrial robot joint. Set joint_valid=true, lower=-180°, upper=180°.
Joint /anymal_c/Geometry/base/LF_HIP/LF_THIGH/LF_HFE: Revolute joint with lower=-Infinity, upper=Infinity. Industrial robot joint, set joint_valid=true, lower=-180°, upper=180°.
Joint /anymal_c/Geometry/base/LF_HIP/LF_HAA: Revolute joint with lower=-Infinity, upper=Infinity. Industrial robot joint, set joint_valid=true, lower=-180°, upper=180°.
Joint /anymal_c/Geometry/base/RF_HIP/RF_THIGH/RF_SHANK/RF_KFE: Revolute joint with lower=-Infinity, upper=Infinity. Industrial robot joint, set joint_valid=true, lower=-180°, upper=180°.
